code_dossier.name: TraceIdFilter.java
code_dossier.description: A Spring HTTP filter component that extracts trace ID from incoming request headers and propagates it through the MDC (Mapped Diagnostic Context) for distributed tracing and logging correlation purposes.

data.detailed_description:
TraceIdFilter is a critical middleware component in the third-party-provider service that implements distributed tracing capabilities. It extends Spring's OncePerRequestFilter to ensure single execution per request, even in complex filter chain scenarios.

The filter operates by:
1. Intercepting all incoming HTTP requests before they reach controllers
2. Extracting the 'X-Trace-Id' header from the request (typically passed from upstream services or API gateways)
3. Storing the trace ID in SLF4J's MDC (Mapped Diagnostic Context), making it available for all logging statements within the request scope
4. Ensuring proper cleanup of the MDC after request processing to prevent memory leaks and cross-request contamination

This pattern is essential for microservices architectures where requests span multiple services, enabling correlation of logs across the entire request lifecycle. The trace ID allows operations teams to track a single request as it flows through various services in a distributed system.

The component follows the standard filter pattern with proper resource management using try-finally blocks, ensuring the MDC is always cleaned up regardless of whether the request succeeds or throws an exception.
responsibilities: Extract and propagate distributed trace ID from HTTP request headers for request correlation
Manage MDC (Mapped Diagnostic Context) lifecycle to enable trace ID visibility in all log statements during request processing
Ensure thread-safe cleanup of trace context after each request to prevent memory leaks and data pollution
Integrate with Spring's filter chain mechanism to provide transparent tracing without modifying business logic
Support observability and debugging in microservices architecture by enabling log aggregation across service boundaries

interfaces.name: doFilterInternal

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: The incoming HTTP servlet request containing headers and request data

parameters.name: response
parameters.description: The HTTP servlet response object for the current request

parameters.name: filterChain
parameters.description: The filter chain to delegate request processing to the next filter or servlet

interfaces.description: Core filter method that extracts trace ID from request header, sets it in MDC, processes the request through the filter chain, and ensures cleanup
